questions.reason: Reason (R): In a reversible adiabatic process, the heat transfer is zero.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): In a reversible adiabatic process, the change in internal energy is zero.
questions.explanation: In a reversible adiabatic process, since the heat transfer is zero, the change in internal energy is also zero due to the first law of thermodynamics, ΔQ = ΔU + ΔW.

questions.reason: Reason (R): The efficiency of an ideal Carnot engine depends on the temperature ratio of the hot and cold reservoirs.
questions.assertion: Assertion (A): The efficiency of an ideal Carnot engine depends on the temperature of the hot reservoir.
questions.explanation: The efficiency of an ideal Carnot engine is given by η = 1 - (Tc/Th), which depends on the temperature ratio of the hot and cold reservoirs, and hence the temperature of the hot reservoir.
